rationale: The citing paper gives Wuyts et al. (2004) a clear functional role in its conceptual framing of multipartner/triadic vertical relationships, not just a background list mention. It appears in the core literature table as prior evidence on supplier-vendor-buyer structure effects, and the authors directly quote Wuyts et al. to justify focusing on a triadic setup (one upstream plus two downstream partners). The citing paper then positions its own contribution as addressing what prior multipartner work had not examined, namely performance implications and boundary conditions of plural versus dyadic alliance structures. This indicates meaningful intellectual carryover from the cited work into the study design and positioning.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Wuyts et al.'s triadic perspective in vertical channels and uses it to justify modeling alliances as one upstream firm with two downstream partners. It extends that line by testing when plural versus dyadic structures improve upstream firm performance and by adding moderators and mechanisms (learning and dependence balancing).
